meanings.definition: the act of making something futile and useless (as by routine)
make vague or obscure or make (an image) less visible
cut a girdle around so as to kill by interrupting the circulation of water and nutrients
make vapid or deprive of spirit
lessen the momentum or velocity of
become lifeless, less lively, intense, or active; lose life, force, or vigor
make less lively, intense, or vigorous; impair in vigor, force, activity, or sensation
convert (metallic mercury) into a grey powder consisting of minute globules, as by shaking with chalk or fatty oil
so lacking in interest as to cause mental weariness; ; ; ; ; ; - Edmund Burke; ; - Mark Twain
examples: a boring evening with uninteresting people
the deadening effect of some routine tasks
a ho-hum speaker who couldn't capture their attention
what an irksome task the writing of long letters is
the tiresome chirping of a cricket
other people's dreams are dreadfully wearisome
